pages.title: History - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: History - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us History Home History Shree RamKrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences (RKI) is managed by the Sarvajanik Education Society (SES) which is about 96 years old educational organization. The word SARVJANIK means -the education is exclusively for all at an affordable cost. The statement on the emblem of our management is “Tamso ma Jyotirmagaya” which means ‘O Lord, Lead us from darkness into light’. RKI fulfills the mission of SES. Ours is a self finance institute established at heart of the Surat city in the year 1999. The institute was started with only one course named B.Sc. Computer Science in year 1999. At that time according to the requirement the institute was started in the building of an engineering college run by the same management. At that time the institute was run using three class rooms and one computer lab. At that time the college was using physics laboratory of adjacent college managed by the same management on sharing base. One more course was started named B.Sc with Microbiology in 2003. At that time the college was using laboratory of physics, chemistry, biology and microbiology of adjacent college managed by the same management on sharing base. After that more new courses were started by the institute named M. Sc. Microbiology and M.Sc. Biotechnology. Due to increased requirement the institute decided to construct new building. In 2007 -2008, a complete new three story building was constructed with the expense of Rs. 2.37 carore approximately. The institute was shifted in new building in May 2008. In the new building all the necessary infrastructure was developed with all the necessary equipments in the director office, administrative office, class rooms and laboratories of various subjects. About Us SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) History Vision, Mission & Values Chairman's Message Principal's Message Working Committee Donors Principals College M.T.B College Campus, B/h P.T Science College, Opp.Chowpati, Athwalines, Surat-395001 Gujarat, India. pages.title: SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) Home SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) Our Management Sarvajanik Education Society, a premier educational institution established in Surat in 1912 with the management of just one secondary school, then known as the English school (Dhingali School – Doll’s School) is today 100 years old and has under its care 35 institutions imparting education from pre-school level to post graduate level covering variety of disciplines including engineering and management education. Society also has under its umbrella two research institutions – one for Languages and social sciences and one for Environmental Engineering. Right from its inception the Sarvajanik Education Society has survived and thrived, solely on the charity of philanthropic institutions and individuals. Its objective as incorporated in its constitution is to spread education among people at affordable cost. Thus, Sarvajanik Education Society is one of those rare institutions, which keep profit motive out of their operative fields and activities. Even then, it has made constant and steady progress. Thanks to the wholehearted support of its well-wishers of this region, in various parts of India and abroad. A very large number of its past students have settled abroad and in acknowledgement of the education they received at Sarvajanik Education Society, continue to extend their help, in every respect. The Sarvajanik Education Society has always enjoyed high reputation, public faith for its noble traditions of honest, transparent and democratic management. It has earned people’s trust that every farthing given to it is well used for the benefit of people and the future generation. The long journey of these 100 years was not smooth. Sarvajanik Education Society witnessed many vicissitudes, particularly in its financial position; and the 2006 unprecedented floods caused the hardest ever blow. However we have fortunately overcome all the repercussion of this natural calamity and set our institutes once again towards academic growth. The unprecedented floods caused irreparable loss to our historically rich and reputed library as major portion of the treacle of its books, masterpieces and rare works of literature were washed out. In the last one and a half decade, the Society has taken a huge stride towards modernizing its education and meaningfully enhanced and widened its magnitude. Considering the future advances in technology for education. The Social needs and expectations in respect of education are ever expanding and newer avenues of development have to be explored for a healthy and socially relevant growth of education for tomorrow. The Sarvajanik Education Society is therefore striving to recognize the changing needs and to organize its affairs accordingly with full confidence as it had in the past, our donors and well-wishers will help the Society with wholehearted generosity in our Endeavor. The Society has inherited glorious past with its monumental buildings and great campuses. Over the century requires repairs and maintenance and so are the campuses. With ever increasing cost this has become an uphill task but we need to do it. Society has embarked upon this renovation project for all its campuses and buildings. T&TV Sarvajanik High School Nanpura, the oldest institute, having celebrated its 125th Anniversary, stands like a new building giving the gothic structure a brand new look. The entire credit goes to the Alumni Association of the School. Society has always tried to keep pace with changing times and the need of the hour is introduction of new technology in education. Society has modernized its schools and the state of the art interactive educational aids have been introduced. Over this journey of 100 years, many dreams have become reality and many are in the pipe line. The journey for education never ends but the quest always continues. New leadership with new ideas and new young team is always eager to implement them. Like Tennyson’s Brook, Men may come and Men may go but Society will continue to spread the light of knowledge time immemorial and will serve the country in shaping the future of thousands of future citizens of India for many centuries to come. Our Institute This college is established & developed with the donations from many organizations, one of them is Shree Ramkrishna Charitable Trust. By providing solid academic foundation, the rigorous curriculum, it prepares you to successfully complete your programme. You will study in State of the art laboratories and lecture rooms. You will also get personalized academic support and guidance of caring faculty for teaching & learning. The college will remain open from 09:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m. Our affiliation The institute is affiliated to Veer Narmad South Gujarat University since 1999 for all the courses time to time till today. With the inception of Sarvajanik University in the year 2021 institute is now one of the constituent colleges of Sarvajanik University.It has already been accredited by National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) as B Grade with 2.67 CGPA and by AAA(KCG Govt. of Gujrat) in 2015 with 2.92 on four point scale. About Us SRKI (Constituent college of Sarvajanik University) History Vision, Mission & Values Chairman's Message Principal's Message Working Committee Donors Principals College M.T.B College Campus, B/h P.T Science College, Opp.Chowpati, Athwalines, Surat-395001 Gujarat, India. T&TV Sarvajanik High School Nanpura, the oldest institute, having celebrated its 125th Anniversary, stands like a new building giving the gothic structure a brand new look. The entire credit goes to the Alumni Association of the School. Society has always tried to keep pace with changing times and the need of the hour is introduction of new technology in education. Society has modernized its schools and the state of the art interactive educational aids have been introduced. Over this journey of 100 years, many dreams have become reality and many are in the pipe line. The journey for education never ends but the quest always continues. New leadership with new ideas and new young team is always eager to implement them. Like Tennyson’s Brook, Men may come and Men may go but Society will continue to spread the light of knowledge time immemorial and will serve the country in

implement them. Like Tennyson’s Brook, Men may come and Men may go but Society will continue to spread the light of knowledge time immemorial and will serve the country in shaping the future of thousands of future citizens of India for many centuries to come. Our Institute This college is established & developed with the donations from many organizations, one of them is Shree Ramkrishna Charitable Trust. By providing solid academic foundation, the rigorous curriculum, it prepares you to successfully complete your programme. You will study in State of the art laboratories and lecture rooms. You will also get personalized academic support and guidance of caring faculty for teaching & learning. The college will remain open from 09:00 a.m. to 06:00 p.m. Our affiliation The institute is affiliated to Veer Narmad South Gujarat University since 1999 for all the courses time to time till today. With the inception of Sarvajanik University in the year 2021 institute is now one of

Narmad South Gujarat University since 1999 for all the courses time to time till today. With the inception of Sarvajanik University in the year 2021 institute is now one of the constituent colleges of Sarvajanik University.It has already been accredited by National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) as B Grade with 2.67 CGPA and by AAA(KCG Govt. of Gujrat) in 2015 with 2.92 on four point scale. pages.title: Chairman's Message - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Chairman's Message - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Chairman's Message Home Chairman's Message Dear All, It was once said that India’s Destiny is being shaped in Four Walls. In four walls of class rooms educational institutes were expected to work together with students and teachers to develop good human resource for the welfare of mankind. However, in the present scenario India’s destiny is being shaped both in and outside four walls. I wish you all a very happy and successful year full of laurels and accolades. I express my sincere thanks for placing your trust and confidence in me to lead 107 old Sarvajanik Education Society (SES) – a task I accept with deepest humility. I welcome all the new members to the Sarvajanikan fraternity and seek the kind support and commitment to steer SES to new vistas and pathways of success. SES primarily being an Educational Institute, teachers are the torch bearers in all the activities and programmes of the Society. It shall be my endeavour to involve all worthy teachers in the ventures of SES with a view to enhance the image of SES and make its presence profoundly felt not only within the region but also in National & International educational arenas. I also strongly believe that we need to make sincere attempts to reach out to the policy makers, Industrial organizations to give our valuable inputs/services on many important policy issues/training programmes. This can be possible only when each one contributes his/her might as experts in the field. Equally significant is the initiative to recognize the young graduates as it is they who are the future leaders of tomorrow. We shall make efforts to seek their greater contribution in the activities of the society. Like any good educational institute, SES take the responsibility of shaping the future of business and society by developing good human resources with putting focus on primary to secondary to tertiary and further into research by fulfilling academic needs of over 30000+ students of this region. I look forward to your suggestions and guidance for all inputs that lead to the continuing progress of SES. I wish you all a great success in all endeavours in the years to come. Let us carry out transition from Teaching to Learning. Chairman, Sarvajanik Education Society. pages.title: Principal's Message - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Principal's Message - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Principal's Message Home Principal's Message Dear Parents, students and well-wishers of the SRKI family- my prayerful greetings to all of you. First and foremost I would like to express my deep sentiments of gratitude to every one of you for your constant support, love and concern towards the college which enables and encourages us to strive hard to carry forward the mission of spreading value-based knowledge to one and all. Education is not an act of acquiring knowledge but learning a skill to lead life and forming one’s personality. This is an ennobling process of growth. I can boldly say that we have excelled in every initiative that we undertook and we have stood together in facing the challenges in realizing quality education. We focus on discovering, developing and drawing out the hidden talents and the magic lying dormant inside all of its students. From academics to co-curricular activities, perseverance and a never-say-die spirit are entrenched in the heart of every student not only making them good students but brilliant human beings. In today’s dynamic world, a 360 degree development and grooming is of supreme importance and through our campus we are creating an environment for future leaders, entrepreneurs and professional who possess skills and aptitudes in an array of functional disciplines. I am very happy with the progress the college has made by imbibing in its students value based education synergized with modern teaching-learning methods to produce a generation of well informed and emotionally sound generation. I am positive that in times to come we will continue this journey with elevated enthusiasm and persistently provide a platform of holistic learning to the young generation of learners. We are partners in learning with our students and seek to deepen their knowledge, provide enriching and innovative learning experiences, while also gently urging our students to come out of their comfort zone to attempt new challenges. Our students graduate with the skills, mind sets and qualities that will best equip them for success Come on let’s give our best and make this institution a modern temple of learning through our diligence, devotion and dedication. Wishing you all the best…!in the world of the future. They are the future leaders and role models of society. pages.title: Donors - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Donors - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Donors Home Donors Brief details about Our Respected donors . Shree Ramkrishna Charitable trust had generously donated Rs. TWENTY FIVE LACKS in the year 1998 to start our Institute, which was very important. In year 2007-2008 the institute received major donation for the construction of new institute building of Rs FIFTY LACKS from Shree Ramkrishna Charitable trust. Shri Govindbhai Dholakiya and Shri Shamjibhai Patel of shree Ramkrishna Charitable trust had played very important role in this donation. In March 2011 the institute received generous donation of Rs. TWENTY FIVE LACKS from Shri Yushufbhai and a name “ SHRI YUSHUFBHAI SHAIKH FAZELHUSEIN ZAVERI WING ” is given to first floor wing of the institute. In September 2012 the Institute received a donation of Rs. NINE LACKS FIFTEEN THOUSAND from KRIBHCO ( KRISHAK BHARTI COOPERATIVE LIMITED, Hazira, Surat ) for the purchase of different kinds of equipments for biotechnology laboratory of the institute. Due to hard efforts from Director Dr. P. B. Desai and Dr. (Mrs.) Kusumben Desai the institute received Rs. TWELVE LACKS from LODGE HAMILTON TRUST for the purchase of High Performance Liquid Chromatography instrument for the research students pursuing their doctoral and post doctoral studies. The institute received donation of Rs. TWENTY FIVE LACKS from SURAT PEOPLE’S MEMORIAL FUND for Microbiology wing in October 2013. We are thankful to all the donors who have donated generously for the construction, development and overall growth of the institute. pages.title: Accrediation - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Accrediation - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Accrediation Home Accrediation Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education & Applied Sciences assessed and accredited by National Accreditation Board for Testing and Calibration Laboratories (NABL), a, constituent board of Quality Council of India (QCI) as per ISO/IEC-17025:2005 as “General Requirements for the Competence of Testing & Calibration Laboratories” in the field of testing in 2018 till 2020. The Knowledge Consortium of Gujarat had accredited Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education & Applied Sciences with CGPA of 2.92 ‘B’ Grade in the year 2015. The National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), Government of India has accredited Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education & Applied Sciences affiliated to VNSGU with CGPA of 2.67 ‘B’ Grade in the year 2011. pages.title: Computer Science - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Computer Science - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Computer Science Home Computer Science Head of department Resources and Facilities Department Activities Student Profile Mr. Jayesh Arvindlal Pushtiwala MCA, NET(Computer Science) Head of department With the new advancements in the field of computers and in a time when there is a boom in the IT industry, the Sarvajanik Education Society introduced B.Sc. (Computer Science), a three year undergraduate course for the tech-savvy youth. Since the inception of this college the department of computer science has been in to existence i.e. from the year 1999. The course provides rigorous foundations of the concepts of Computer Science and Information Technology. In the final year, students also get an opportunity to do project work. Hence the combination of the concepts and training of software tools equip the students to adapt to ever-changing technology.In 2010, the department started offering a two years, post graduation level degree course, M.Sc. (Computer Application). The college is contributing in its own inimitable way to the development of Computer science by offering the courses with the help of efficient and highly qualified teachers and through a well-equipped computer lab.Every year the department is conducting various competitions like software programming, seminar and poster competitions for UG and PG students. Server, Apache Server, Glassfish Server, Wordpress Open source WPS Office, LibreOffice ACCESS Servers in computer departments: Primary domain controller: A PDC is prepared to provide restricted login access to the students and staff members of the institute. It has been synchronized with network storage to keep a safe copy of all important data. Backup domain controller: A BDC is prepared to provide backbone support to the primary domain controller. Oracle Server: An ORACLE database server is installed and configured to provide database service across the network. A node requires only client component to establish connectivity. Access of devices for different users Specific policy based logins in active directory of windows server 2008 are created for all users to provide relevant access to the student, faculties and others. Rights to the access network attached devices including server and printer are also configured using group policy. Wi-Fi and Internet Access: Computers and IT equipments at various locations of institute are connected in network environment using four Wi-fi access points at four floors. The institute has subscribed for 19.2 Mbps of broadband connection bandwidth to cater the need of internet usage of staff members and students. Computer department is managing the provision of internet connectivity to the institute student and staff through wired and Wi-Fi network. Internet connectivity is provided to entire campus including all the departments, class rooms, library and laboratory through four Wi-fi access points. Proxy server is used to manage and control the internet traffic The internet can be used by students and faculties for teaching and learning purpose in controlled environment. Network attached storage The institute is having a NAS server that server that manages and maintains the institutional data at the centralized location for shared access, backup and retrieval. NAS server is kept in server room of computer lab with 2 TB capacity of storage and RAID facility. The regular scheduled backup of data is taken daily. User wise data can be stored in secured mode and can be access at any location through network. User wise data access has been restricted through custom configuration. Purchase and maintenance Purchase process of IT peripherals. The management has implemented central purchase process system by inviting tenders from different vendors. Institutional needs are collected along with budgetary provision and preferred brands / configurations and tenders are floated and quotations are invited. Comparative statement is prepared and then negotiation is done on technical, financial and support service bases. Finally purchase order is placed and payment is done after installation and verification of the equipment. Maintenance and annual maintenance contract (AMC). For smooth operations of all IT infrastructure and maximum up-time, routine in-house maintenance is carried out by network support team including computer lab assistant. Annual maintenance contract is given for continuous support to solve various issues and problems to ensure minimum down time. Logbook is maintained various places to record the problem details. A dead stock register is maintained to keep record of all the equipment. Services extended by computer department: Routine installation and maintenance work of IT equipment of the institute. Installation and maintenance of the software. Management and maintenance of NAS server. Management and maintenance of internet provision through wired and Wi-Fi network. Each classrooms and computer labs are equipped with ceiling mount projectors. Maintenance of these projectors is carried out by computer department. 2015-16 2016-17 2017-18 2018-19 2019-20 2020-21 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24 List of Activity Report 2015-16 Report of Activities of Department of Computer Science Year 2015-16 List of Activity Report 2016-17 Report of Activities of Department of Computer Science Year 2016-17 List of Activity Report 2017-18 Report of Activities of Department of Computer Science Year 2017-18 List of Activity Report 2018-19 Report of Activities of Department of Computer Science Year 2018-19 List of Activity Report 2019-20 Report of Expert Lecture on Trends in Web Development Applications Report of Expert Lecture on Analysis of Algorithm Report On Two Days Workshop On IOT Application Using Arduino and Rasberry PI List of Activity Report 2020-21 Report of Three days workshop on ReactJS (Online) List of Activity Report 2021-22 Report on Webinar AI & ML Overview Report on Webinar Software Engineering: Various Sectors, Roles & Responsibilities Report of Intercollege Poster Presentation competition on "Digital India" List of Activity Report 2022-23 Report of Seminar on “Full Stack Development” Report of Seminar on “How to Prevent API Abuse” Report of Seminar on “Contact manager using JS and Fetch API” List of Activity Report 2023-24 Report of Workshop on “Unveilling the secrets of online protection: Cyber Security” Report on Seminar on “Database Model and Query Processing” Report of Workshop on “Web 3.0 and Blockchain” Report of One day workshop on “Diving Deeper into AI Trends : Explainable AI with Generative AI Tools and Techniques” Report of Session on “Mastering Web Development using Django” Student Strength (2025-26) Category Computer Science. (Computer Science), a three year undergraduate course for the tech-savvy youth. Since the inception of this college the department of computer science has been in to existence i.e. from the year 1999. The course provides rigorous foundations of the concepts of Computer Science and Information Technology. In the final year, students also get an opportunity to do project work. Hence the combination of the concepts and training of software tools equip the students to adapt to ever-changing technology.In 2010, the department

also get an opportunity to do project work. Hence the combination of the concepts and training of software tools equip the students to adapt to ever-changing technology.In 2010, the department started offering a two years, post graduation level degree course, M.Sc. (Computer Application). The college is contributing in its own inimitable way to the development of Computer science by offering the courses with the help of efficient and highly qualified teachers and through a well-equipped computer lab.Every year the department is conducting various competitions like software programming, seminar and poster competitions for UG and PG students. 2 years warranty 01 Hitachi Make 12 TB Enterprise SATA 3.5" HDD for NAS with 5 years Warranty 02 Licensed Software License software Microsoft Azure Dev Tools for Teaching (MSDN Academic Alliance) (Windows servers, Windows OS 8,10, Visual Studio, SQL Server, MS Access, MS Office) 0 1 Antivirus Seqrite Endpoint Security 7.4 admin console with clients 3 08 Open Source Software / Environment Open source OS : Ubuntu 18.04 Open source Softwares: Netbeans 8.0, Oracle 11g Express Edition, Open source JDK version 11.1, DosBox Emmulator, TC Open source Android Studio 3.2, Visual Code, Python Jupyter Notebook Open source Lamp Server, Apache Server, Glassfish Server, Wordpress Open source WPS Office, LibreOffice ACCESS Servers in computer departments: Primary domain controller: A PDC is prepared to provide restricted login access to the students and staff members of the institute. It has been synchronized with network storage to keep a safe copy of all important data. Backup domain controller: A BDC is prepared to

and staff members of the institute. It has been synchronized with network storage to keep a safe copy of all important data. Backup domain controller: A BDC is prepared to provide backbone support to the primary domain controller. Oracle Server: An ORACLE database server is installed and configured to provide database service across the network. A node requires only client component to establish connectivity. Access of devices for different users Specific policy based logins in active directory of windows server 2008 are created for all users to provide relevant access to the student, faculties and others. Rights to the access network attached devices including server and printer are also configured using group policy. Wi-Fi and Internet Access: Computers and IT equipments at various locations of institute are connected in network environment using four Wi-fi access points at four floors. The institute has subscribed for 19.2 Mbps of broadband connection bandwidth to cater the need of internet usage of staff members

environment using four Wi-fi access points at four floors. The institute has subscribed for 19.2 Mbps of broadband connection bandwidth to cater the need of internet usage of staff members and students. Computer department is managing the provision of internet connectivity to the institute student and staff through wired and Wi-Fi network. Internet connectivity is provided to entire campus including all the departments, class rooms, library and laboratory through four Wi-fi access points. Proxy server is used to manage and control the internet traffic The internet can be used by students and faculties for teaching and learning purpose in controlled environment. Network attached storage The institute is having a NAS server that server that manages and maintains the institutional data at the centralized location for shared access, backup and retrieval. NAS server is kept in server room of computer lab with 2 TB capacity of storage and RAID facility. The regular scheduled backup of data is taken daily. User wise

NAS server is kept in server room of computer lab with 2 TB capacity of storage and RAID facility. The regular scheduled backup of data is taken daily. User wise data can be stored in secured mode and can be access at any location through network. User wise data access has been restricted through custom configuration. Purchase and maintenance Purchase process of IT peripherals. The management has implemented central purchase process system by inviting tenders from different vendors. Institutional needs are collected along with budgetary provision and preferred brands / configurations and tenders are floated and quotations are invited. Comparative statement is prepared and then negotiation is done on technical, financial and support service bases. Finally purchase order is placed and payment is done after installation and verification of the equipment. Maintenance and annual maintenance contract (AMC). For smooth operations of all IT infrastructure and maximum up-time, routine in-house maintenance is carried out by network support team including computer lab

equipment. Maintenance and annual maintenance contract (AMC). For smooth operations of all IT infrastructure and maximum up-time, routine in-house maintenance is carried out by network support team including computer lab assistant. Annual maintenance contract is given for continuous support to solve various issues and problems to ensure minimum down time. Logbook is maintained various places to record the problem details. A dead stock register is maintained to keep record of all the equipment. Services extended by computer department: Routine installation and maintenance work of IT equipment of the institute. Installation and maintenance of the software. Management and maintenance of NAS server. Management and maintenance of internet provision through wired and Wi-Fi network. Each classrooms and computer labs are equipped with ceiling mount projectors. Maintenance of these projectors is carried out by computer department. 2015-16 2016-17 2017-18 2018-19 2019-20 2020-21 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24 List of Activity Report 2015-16 Report of Activities of Department of Computer Science Year 2015-16 List of Activity

(Microbiology), Ph.D. (Microbiology) Head Of Department The department of microbiology was established in 2002, and has already shown its mark in the teaching microbiology subject. At the B.Sc. Degree course the department offer microbiology as Major subject (8 papers having 10 units each) and Chemistry as Minor subject (four papers with 8 units each). More than 250 students have successfully completed the university degree course. Our students are university rankers in the subject of microbiology for three years. Several students passing from this department have qualified CSTR-UGC-NET/GATE examinations. Few of them are working for their Ph.D. degree in different universities and research institutes in India and abroad. Rest of the successful candidates of last batch have been placed in different jobs. Year of establishment BSc. Microbiology: June 2002 MSc. (Microbiology), Ph.D. (Microbiology) Head Of Department The department of microbiology was established in 2002, and has already shown its mark in the teaching microbiology subject. At the B.Sc. Degree course the department offer microbiology as Major subject (8 papers having 10 units each) and Chemistry as Minor subject (four papers with 8 units each). More than 250 students have successfully completed the university degree course. Our students are university rankers in the subject of microbiology for three years. Several students passing from this department have qualified CSTR-UGC-NET/GATE examinations. pages.text: Biotechnology - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Biotechnology Home Biotechnology Head of department Resources and Facilities Department Activities Student Profile Mr.Jay Babulal Bergi M.Sc(MicroBiology),NET(Life Science), PH.D. Head Of Department Biotechnology in one form or other has flourished since prehistoric times. When the first human beings realized that they could plant crops, breed their own animals and convert milk into cheese or yogurt they learned to use biotechnology. Biotechnology is a research oriented applied science , a combination of Biology and Technology. It is based on the applications of sound fundamental knowledge of wide variety of subjects like Zoology, Botany, Genetics, Biochemistry , Microbiology, Immunology, Virology, Chemistry and Engineering. It contributes to the areas like Pharmaceutical Production, Health and Medicine, Agriculture and Animal Husbandry, Environment quality, Pollution management, Seed Technology, etc. Biotechnology is the use of living things, especially cells and bacteria in industrial process. There is a great scope in this field as the demands for biotechnologist are growing in India as well as abroad. Considering this growing significance of this subject Sarvajanik Education Society started an integrated M.Sc. course in biotechnology. Department of biotechnology established at RKI in 2008. M.Sc. Biotechnology program is a 5 years B.Sc. + M.Sc. integrated program after 12 th Science. Students can leave the program after three years while securing B.Sc. degree. Students passing B.Sc. degree in Biotechnology, Microbiology and Biochemistry can join M.Sc. program from Semester 7. Outstanding Features: Highly qualified, efficient, experienced and multi-talented teaching and technical staff Advanced laboratories with high-end imported instruments Excellent collection of books, e-resources in library State of art infrastructure support with Wi-Fi enabled campus Department Faculty Photo Name Designation Qualification Specialization Joining Date Dr. Nehal Dilipbhai Shah Asst. Skill Based: 1 BT:S-4001 Essential Skills for Biopharmaceutical Industry 2 BT:S-4002 Essential Skills for Bio-services and Bio-Agri Industries 3 BT:S-4003 Essential Skills for Clinical Laboratories 4 BT:S-4004 Skill Enhancement Laboratory Work Laboratories Each laboratory of biotechnology department enriched with advanced instruments and is able to support regular experiments, dissertation work as well as high-end research. Sophisticated instruments needed for experiments and research are accessible in central instrumentation facility of the college. Students are given precious opportunity of hands-on use of all these instruments. Department has three dedicated state of the art laboratories for following subjects. Lab-1 Microbial Biotechnology (To study production of pharmaceutical and other industrial compounds at laboratory level.) Lab 2 – Plant Tissue Culture (To study artificial cultivation of improved plants from various tissue implants.) Lab 3 – Animal Cell Culture (Cultivation of animal cells in laboratory for studies of genetic, toxicology and cancer formation, diagnosis and treatment of various diseases.) Department uses four other laboratories for following subjects on sharing basis with allied departments of the same institute. · Bioinformatics (To study biological research data generation, storage and manipulation) · Microbiology (To develop essential skills for applied microbiology) · Biochemistry (To study chemical aspects of living organisms and skills to manipulate it) · Botany and Zoology (To develop essential skills for applied botany and zoology) Research: Faculties of department are actively inclined towards research. Faculties have submitted 8 research proposals to various government grant agencies.Out of this one Major Research Project is already been approved and granted. Two faculties of department are actively supervising research students for M.Phil. degree. All the faculties have participated in workshops, seminars and conferences at regular basis. Two of the faculties have presented their research work at international level in countries like USA, Norway and China. College has subscribed for good quality international research journals including the best in subject ‘Nature Biotechnology’. Institute is the member of INFLIBINET scheme for availing online research articles. 2016-17 2017-18 2018-19 2019-20 2020-21 2021-22 List of Activity Report 2016-17 World Breast Feeding Day From 4th – 6th August,2016 Worlds Lion Day on 10th August,2016 Awareness on Mental Health & Suicide on 22th August,2016 Guest Lecture on “Planet Hormones” on 3rd Sep,2016 Guest Lecture on “Biology of Dormancy” on 9th Sep,2016 Education Tour at Sumul Dairy on 8th Sep,2016 Education Tour at Sewage Treatment Plant, Variav-Kosad, Surat Visit to Educational Field Trip at West Coast Frozen Food Pvt. Head Of Department Biotechnology in one form or other has flourished since prehistoric times. When the first human beings realized that they could plant crops, breed their own animals and convert milk into cheese or yogurt they learned to use biotechnology. Biotechnology is a research oriented applied science , a combination of Biology and Technology. It is based on the applications of sound fundamental knowledge of wide variety of subjects like Zoology, Botany, Genetics, Biochemistry , Microbiology, Immunology, Virology, Chemistry and Engineering. It contributes to the areas like Pharmaceutical Production, Health and Medicine, Agriculture and Animal Husbandry, Environment quality, Pollution management, Seed Technology, etc. Biotechnology is the use of living things, especially cells and bacteria in

areas like Pharmaceutical Production, Health and Medicine, Agriculture and Animal Husbandry, Environment quality, Pollution management, Seed Technology, etc. Biotechnology is the use of living things, especially cells and bacteria in industrial process. There is a great scope in this field as the demands for biotechnologist are growing in India as well as abroad. Considering this growing significance of this subject Sarvajanik Education Society started an integrated M.Sc. course in biotechnology. Department of biotechnology established at RKI in 2008. M.Sc. Biotechnology program is a 5 years B.Sc. + M.Sc. integrated program after 12 th Science. Students can leave the program after three years while securing B.Sc. degree. Students passing B.Sc. degree in Biotechnology, Microbiology and Biochemistry can join M.Sc. program from Semester 7. Department uses four other laboratories for following subjects on sharing basis with allied departments of the same institute. · Bioinformatics (To study biological research data generation, storage and manipulation) · Microbiology (To develop essential skills for applied microbiology) · Biochemistry

basis with allied departments of the same institute. · Bioinformatics (To study biological research data generation, storage and manipulation) · Microbiology (To develop essential skills for applied microbiology) · Biochemistry (To study chemical aspects of living organisms and skills to manipulate it) · Botany and Zoology (To develop essential skills for applied botany and zoology) Research: Faculties of department are actively inclined towards research. Faculties have submitted 8 research proposals to various government grant agencies.Out of this one Major Research Project is already been approved and granted. Two faculties of department are actively supervising research students for M.Phil. degree. All the faculties have participated in workshops, seminars and conferences at regular basis. Two of the faculties have presented their research work at international level in countries like USA, Norway and China. College has subscribed for good quality international research journals including the best in subject ‘Nature Biotechnology’. Institute is the member of INFLIBINET scheme for availing online research articles. 2016-17

Environmental Science curriculum provides students with opportunities to gain extensive hands-on experience with these scientific tools and facilities through lab-based coursework and research projects. A wide variety of scientific equipment is used to analyse environmental samples. Our laboratories are equipped with high-quality, state-of-art experimental facilities enabling research at the forefront of science and technology. The department maintains fully-calibrated instruments, like CoD digesters, binocular microscopes, UV-Vis spectrophotometer, High volume samplers etc. which are available to students along with training and troubleshooting support. Facilities provide standard equipments and expertise, which support in-house (intramural) research activities at the institute. Department also uses four other laboratories for following subjects on sharing basis with various departments of the same institute. Ecoinformatics and Ecological Modelling (To study ecological data generation and analysis) Image analysis, GPS and qGIS (To study frontier aspects of RS and GIS technology) Microbiology (To develop essential skills for applied microbiology) Physics (To study energy balance aspects of various energy resources) Botany and Zoology (To develop essential skills for applied botany and zoology) Research: The Department has an active research program in various subdomains of Environment Science. Faculties have taken up various intramural and students’ dissertation projects in Environmental Science. Two faculties of department are actively supervising research students for M.Phil. and Ph. D. degree. Faculties and students are encouraged to participate and present their work in workshops, seminars and conferences at regular basis. Faculties are also involved in consultancy project and part of GPCB/CPCB audit team. College has subscribed for good quality international research journals and is the member of INFLIBINET scheme for availing online research articles. The department is equipped with following instruments: No. Name of Instruments Make/Company 1. Binocular Microscope Uni lab 2. Monoculor microscope Uni lab 3. COD Digester Durga Scientific 4. Rotary flask shaker 25 flask capacity Durga Scientific IIRS-ISRO-EDUSAT Centre: The Indian Institute of Remote Sensing is a leading education and research institute for Remote Sensing, Geoinformatics and GPS Technology in area of Disaster Management, Resource identification and distribution and smart city development. IIRS Outreach Program focusses on strengthening the Academia in Space Technology & Its Applications using Online Learning Platforms. With the aim to promote geospatial technologies through academia- industry interface, various outreach programs were organized by IIRS. The contents were delivered across the nation using Online Learning Platforms by experts. Our institute has participated in the outreach program which has increase understanding and application of remote sensing and GIS in various field. Environmental Science department has been recognized by Indian Institute of Remote Sensing (IIRS), Dehradun as the Nodal Centre for the EDUSAT based distance learning programme on remote sensing, GIS and GPS for organizing regularly this programme from 2016 onwards. The course has empowered the students to gain knowledge of RS, GIS and GNSS technologies. It also held them to develop image analysis and data handling. By enrolling into the course the participants are enable to carry out many new research paradigm based on geo spatial data. Annual Course Calendar IIRS Distance Learning Programme – 2020 For further details please contact Dr. Bhavin Bhatt Co-Ordinator ISRO-IIRS EDUSAT program Assistant Professor, Environmental Science Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Sarvajanik Education Society Surat bhavin.bhatt@srki.ac.in RIO Program In June 2012, Brazil hosted United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development (UNCSD), popularly known as Rio+20. Rio+20 was 10-day long mega-conference organized by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs and saw participation from over 190 countries. Rio+20 culminated with the A/RES/66/288 – the future we want declaration. IARC launched the RIO Program (2012-2022) during the Rio+20 Earth Summit on June 20, 2012. The main campaign was called, One Million Rios. The launch was underpinned by the idea “Bringing Rio Home.” Rio+22 UNSE4ALL India Program The focus area of the 2014-2015 Rio+22 program was Sustainable Energy. It was a National Level effort in the form of a short-term co-curricular program for students. It was in synergy with United Nations General Assembly (A/RES/65/151) declared Decade of Sustainable Energy for All. Rio+23 WHS India Program The focus area of RIO+23 Program 2016-17 was to build momentum towards and beyond United Nations World Humanitarian Summit. It was in synergy with the principles of United Nations General Assembly Resolution 46/182. Rio+24 IDRC India Program The focus of Rio+24 IDRC India Program was based on the objectives of International Decade for People of African Descent and United Nations International Decade for the Rapprochement of Cultures (IDRC). It was in synergy with the principles of United Nations General Assembly Resolution Res 67/104 & 68/237. RIO+25: UN Water Action Decade India Program RIO+25 UN Water Action Decade India Program 2019-20 is part of the prestigious RIO Program (2012-2022). RIO+25 Program is in sync with the objectives of United Nations Water Action Decade. Details of Rio25+ programme For further details please contact Dr. Pratik Patel Co-Ordinator RIO program Assistant Professor, Environmental Science Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Sarvajanik Education Society Surat 2015-16 2016-17 2017-18 2018-19 2019-20 2025-26 Activity Report 2015-16 Activity Report 2016-17 Activity Report 2017-18 Activity Report 2018-19 List of Activity Report 2019-20 one day orientation programme - cleaner productions biodiversity park report - swachh survekshan 2020 citizen feedback awareness programme guest lecture on - entrepreneurship for beginners industrial visit - educational trip educational trip smc biodiversity park - althan - surat and sajjan india and nctl etps, ankleshwar - report_es Crane Conservation Report List of Activity During CoVid - 19 Lockdown (sorted according to lock-down version) Report of departmental activities during CoVid-19 Lock-down 1.0 Departmental activities during CoVid-19 Lock-down 2.0 List of Activity Report 2025-26 Industrial (Education) Visit at Common Effluent Treatment Plant (CETP) Student Strength 2025-26 Category Environmental Science B.Sc. Rotary flask shaker 25 flask capacity Durga Scientific IIRS-ISRO-EDUSAT Centre: The Indian Institute of Remote Sensing is a leading education and research institute for Remote Sensing, Geoinformatics and GPS Technology in area of Disaster Management, Resource identification and distribution and smart city development. IIRS Outreach Program focusses on strengthening the Academia in Space

for Remote Sensing, Geoinformatics and GPS Technology in area of Disaster Management, Resource identification and distribution and smart city development. IIRS Outreach Program focusses on strengthening the Academia in Space Technology & Its Applications using Online Learning Platforms. With the aim to promote geospatial technologies through academia- industry interface, various outreach programs were organized by IIRS. The contents were delivered across the nation using Online Learning Platforms by experts. Our institute has participated in the outreach program which has increase understanding and application of remote sensing and GIS in various field. Environmental Science department has been recognized by Indian Institute of Remote Sensing (IIRS), Dehradun as the Nodal Centre for the EDUSAT based distance learning programme on remote sensing, GIS and GPS for organizing regularly this programme from 2016 onwards. The course has empowered the students to gain knowledge of RS, GIS and GNSS technologies. It also held them to develop image analysis and data handling. By enrolling into the

The course has empowered the students to gain knowledge of RS, GIS and GNSS technologies. It also held them to develop image analysis and data handling. By enrolling into the course the participants are enable to carry out many new research paradigm based on geo spatial data. Annual Course Calendar IIRS Distance Learning Programme – 2020 For further details please contact Dr. Bhavin Bhatt Co-Ordinator ISRO-IIRS EDUSAT program Assistant Professor, Environmental Science Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Sarvajanik Education Society Surat bhavin.bhatt@srki.ac.in RIO Program In June 2012, Brazil hosted United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development (UNCSD), popularly known as Rio+20. Rio+20 was 10-day long mega-conference organized by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs and saw participation from over 190 countries. Rio+20 culminated with the A/RES/66/288 – the future we want declaration. IARC launched the RIO Program (2012-2022) during the Rio+20 Earth Summit on June 20, 2012. The main campaign was called, One Million

the A/RES/66/288 – the future we want declaration. IARC launched the RIO Program (2012-2022) during the Rio+20 Earth Summit on June 20, 2012. The main campaign was called, One Million Rios. The launch was underpinned by the idea “Bringing Rio Home.” Rio+22 UNSE4ALL India Program The focus area of the 2014-2015 Rio+22 program was Sustainable Energy. It was a National Level effort in the form of a short-term co-curricular program for students. It was in synergy with United Nations General Assembly (A/RES/65/151) declared Decade of Sustainable Energy for All. Rio+23 WHS India Program The focus area of RIO+23 Program 2016-17 was to build momentum towards and beyond United Nations World Humanitarian Summit. It was in synergy with the principles of United Nations General Assembly Resolution 46/182. Rio+24 IDRC India Program The focus of Rio+24 IDRC India Program was based on the objectives of International Decade for People of African Descent and United Nations International Decade for the Rapprochement of Cultures

The focus of Rio+24 IDRC India Program was based on the objectives of International Decade for People of African Descent and United Nations International Decade for the Rapprochement of Cultures (IDRC). It was in synergy with the principles of United Nations General Assembly Resolution Res 67/104 & 68/237. RIO+25: UN Water Action Decade India Program RIO+25 UN Water Action Decade India Program 2019-20 is part of the prestigious RIO Program (2012-2022). RIO+25 Program is in sync with the objectives of United Nations Water Action Decade. Desai M.Sc., Ph.D.(Organic Chemistry) Head Of Department Chemistry has often been referred to as “The Central Science”. Knowledge of the properties and behavior of atoms and molecules is crucial to our understanding of medicine, biological systems, neuroscience, nanotechnology and environmental science The Chemistry Department offers courses in the traditional major areas of chemistry, as well as in interdisciplinary areas, such as biochemistry, analytical chemistry etc.The Department of the Chemistry was established at the inception of B Sc microbiology course. The rapidly developing fields of Biotechnology, Microbiology and Environmental Science need a student with a strong foundation in Chemistry.So chemistry is taught as one of the subject in all these courses. To develop student talent in order to make a difference in these areas,the Chemistry Department provides students with an excellent, comprehensive education of the principles of chemistry, including a rich laboratory experience within the curriculum. The Chemistry Lab is well equipped with all the facilities required for the working of students.It has analytical based instruments like pH meter, colorimeter, conductometer, polarimeter, dipital balance. pages.title: Laboratory - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Laboratory - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Laboratory Home Laboratory Computer Lab Microbiology Lab Biotechnology Lab Physics & Chemistry Lab lab4 lab10 lab2 lab5 lab6 Previous Next We have a well-equipped two Computer Laboratories with latest technology’s hardware and all needed software we provide separate computer to each student to develop excellent computing skill. 110 computer systems and four server systems are kept in the computer departmental laboratory. All the computer systems are connected through structured network and internet. The Network is supported with four latest Servers and three managed network switches. Computer lab is equipped with various license software like MSDN Academic Alliance (which includes almost all Microsoft Products) and Quick Heal Antivirus Software. Moreover the department laboratory is also equipped with three dot matrix printer, one laser jet printer, one scanner and four UPS. A ceiling mounted projector is placed in the computer laboratory, which is utilized for demonstration. Moreover the class rooms are also equipped with ceiling mounted projectors. Furthermore, these classrooms are connected to internet and computer lab via Wi-Fi Network. The department is provided three laptop computer for demonstrating the concepts and theories in the class rooms using above mentioned ICT tools. We have two microbiology laboratories for B.Sc. & M.Sc. Programme. The microbiology department proudly boasts on its state of the art laboratory. All the laboratories are well equipped with all modern / Advance instruments required to support experiments as per formal curriculum. Not getting restricted to the curriculum of course, the laboratory is designed with a vision to support research also. Facilities in microbiology laboratory can support research in fields like Environmental Microbiology, Clinical Microbiology, Fermentation Technology and Immunology. Technical skills of students will surely hone in cozy environment of microbiology laboratory. Bioinformatics and Biostatics lab is provided with laptops supported by needy software & servers. Laboratories like Microbial biotechnology Lab, Bioprocess Laboratory, Plant Tissue Culture Lab, Cell Biology & Genetic Engineering Lab are equipped with all modern instruments required to support experiments as per formal curriculum. The laboratories are designed with a vision to support research also. Bioinformatics & Biostatics lab is provided with laptops supported by needy software and servers.. Physics laboratory is utilized for the students of first year Computer Science. Similarly chemistry laboratory is utilized for the students of first and second year of Microbiology. Campus NCC Office Laboratory Library Seminar Hall Playground Hostel Canteen Sports College M.T.B College Campus, B/h P.T Science College, Opp.Chowpati, Athwalines, Surat-395001 Gujarat, India. pages.title: Library - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Library - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Library Home Library lib1 lib2 lib3 lib4 Previous Next Introducing SRKI Library: The Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Library is a valuable resource for students, researchers and faculties of Computer science, Microbiology, Biotechnology and Environmental Science. From 1999 to till date the library has built a strong collection of books, current subscription to national and international journals and newspapers, and many other resources like student's project reports, research thesis, CDs, DVDs etc. Considering the importance of computerization of library, our library is automated by using "SOUL" software. Institution library has a membership of BCIL- Biotechnology Club, INFLIBNET-N-LIST (e-resources) and National Environmental Science Academy. Students get library assignments as per their curriculum. They refer the library books, reference books; journals etc. during this period and get credit as per their credit based curriculum. We organize book exhibition every year. Books of various subjects and general books for enhancing the reading habit of students are kept for exhibition. We have different section of Reference books, General Books, Women Empowerment & Competitive Exams. Library Hours: Monday to Friday: 10:00 am to 5:00pm Saturday: 10:00 am to 2:00pm Contact us: Dipali Patel (Library Clerk) Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education &Apllied Sciences, Library Address: MTB College Campus, B/H PT Science College, Opp. Chaupati, Athwalines, Surat-395001. Phone: 0261-2240172, E-Mail: library@srki.ac.in Library at a Glance:  Total No. Books available in Library- 8786  Total No. National & international Journals available in Library- 11  Total No. Magazine available in Library-11  Total No. CD/DVD available in Library- 658  Total No. Newspaper/letter available in Library- 07  Total No. Project Report available in Library- 913  Total No. Research Thesis available in Library- 102 Useful Links for E-Books through N-List: E-resources through N-List: E-Books Cambridge Books Online [1800 titles] E-brary [185000+ titles] EBSCoHost-Net Library [936 titles] Hindustan Book Agency [65+ titles] Institute of South East Asian Studies(ISEAS) Books [382+ titles] Oxford Scholarship [1402+ titles] Springer eBooks [2300 titles] Sage Publication eBooks [1000 titles] Taylor Francis eBooks [1800 titles] Myilibrary-McGraw Hill [1124 titles] South Asia Archive [through NDL] World e-Books Library [Now Available through ND] E-Journals (Fulltext) American Institute of Physics [18 titles] Annual Reviews [33 titles] Economic and Political Weekly (EPW) [1 title] Discontinued from 2024 Indian Journals [180+ titles] Institute of Physics [46 titles] JSTOR [3000+ titles] Oxford University Press [262 titles] Royal Society of Chemistry [29 titles] EBSCO - OmniFile Full Text Mega [3000+ titles] Cambridge University Press [224 titles] (2010-2016) ISID [Database] Useful Links for Competitive Exams: UPSC: Union Public Service Commission http://www.upsc.gov.in/ Civil Services India http://www.civilserviceindia.com/ GPSC: Gujarat Public Service Commission http://www.gpsc.gujarat.gov.in/ OJAS: Online Job Application System http://ojas.gujarat.gov.in/ UGC- NET https://ugcnet nta.nic.in/ CSIR-NET for Life Science https://csirnet.nta.nic.in/ Gujarat State Eligibility Test : http://www.gujaratset.ac.in/ Current Affairs-General Knowledge https://www.gktoday.in Library Services and Facilities: In general, the Library offers following facilities and services: Library Membership Facility Document Borrowing Facility Reference, Referral and Information Services. Reservation of Books Facility to borrow reading materials in open access environment Use of special collections wherever they are available. Bibliographical Services wherever they are available. Use of Project Reports as per the conditions. Free internet access for student & Staff Extra Activities: Orientation program for fresher. Book Exhibition. "Thought of the Day" on separate notice board. User’s suggestion. Rules: Compulsory to have Identity Card. Maximum two Books allow at a time for one week. Fine for late submission of books for first week is Rs.1/- per day and after that Rs. 3/- per day. The student is liable to replace the book if any book is damaged or lost. Project report, seminar report, reference books and Question paper sets will not allow taking outside the library. Silence must be observed Mobile strictly prohibited . pages.title: Seminar Hall - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences
pages.text: Seminar Hall - Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences Shree Ramkrishna Institute of Computer Education and Applied Sciences 0261-2240170,0261-2240172,7228018498,7228018499,7228018500,7228018497 7228018496 info@srki.ac.in Contact Us Seminar Hall Home Seminar Hall Many Co-curricular activities / competitions are organized to promote multi-dimensional growth of students. For these events we have common facilities of two auditoriums at our sister institute Sir P.T. Science College & M.T.B. Arts College. Participation of students is encouraged in various programmes competitions, seminars and conferences at different levels. In-house training session for cultural and other competitions like story writing, drawing, poetry, solo dance, group dance, singing and drama competition are organized by various committees of institute in G6 hall.
